commands.review_summary:
The branch changes `packages/terminal-core/src/note.ts` so `note()` wraps once with OpenClaw, then calls Clack with a widened virtual output stream to avoid Clack re-wrapping copy-sensitive tokens.

PR surface: Source +5. Total +5 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main routes session-lock paths through the shared `note()` helper, and pinned Clack 1.4.0 hard-wraps note content using the output columns. I did not run a full `openclaw doctor` scenario in this read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed: this is an active, narrow implementation PR with no discrete automated repair finding.

target.title: fix(note): prevent clack from re-breaking copy-sensitive tokens
